list.title: Filters
list.loadError: An error has occurred loading filters.
list.copyFailed: Failed to copy JSON to clipboard.
list.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this filter? This action cannot be undone.
list.autoUpdatedTitle: This filter is automatically updated by a list
list.noActionsDefined: No actions defined. Set up actions to enable snatching.
list.noActionsEnabled: You need to enable at least one action in the filter otherwise you will not get any snatches.

details.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this filter? This action cannot be undone.

importer.title: Import filter (in JSON or autodl-irssi format)
importer.jsonImportFailed: Failed to import JSON data. Information logged to console.
importer.invalidFormat: Cannot import given filter -- it is neither in JSON or autodl-irssi format.
importer.autodlImportFailed: Failed to import autodl-irssi filter '{{name}}'. Information logged to console.
importer.autodlImportedPartial: {{success}}/{{total}} filters imported successfully. See console for details.
importer.placeholder: Paste your filter data here (either autobrr JSON format or your entire autodl-irssi config)

general.announceTypesTooltip: NEW! Match releases which contain any of the selected announce types.
general.rulesSubtitle: Specify rules on how torrents should be handled/selected.
general.sizeTooltip: Supports units such as MB, MiB, GB, etc.
general.delayPlaceholder: Number of seconds to delay actions
general.delayTooltip: Number of seconds to wait before running actions.
general.priorityTooltip: Filters are checked in order of priority. Higher number = higher priority.
general.maxDownloadsTooltip: Number of max downloads as specified by the respective unit.
general.maxDownloadsPerTooltip: The unit of time for counting the maximum downloads per filter.

actionsSection.title: Actions
actionsSection.subtitle: Add to download clients or run custom commands.
actionsSection.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this action? This action cannot be undone.
actionsSection.actionSubtitle: Define the download client for your action and its name
actionsSection.actionTypeTooltip: Select the action type for this action.
actionsSection.name: Name

notificationsSection.title: Filter Notifications
notificationsSection.subtitle: Configure which notifications should be sent for this filter. These override global notification settings.
notificationsSection.empty: No filter-specific notifications configured. Global notifications will be used.
notificationsSection.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this notification? This action cannot be undone.
notificationsSection.notificationSubtitle: Select the notification service and events to trigger
notificationsSection.notificationServiceTooltip: Select the notification service to use for this filter.
notificationsSection.muteFilterDesc: Disable all notifications for this filter. When muted, no notifications will be sent for releases matched by this filter.
notificationsSection.mutedInfo: Filter muted - overrides all global notification settings
notificationsSection.pushApprovedDesc: Send notification when release is successfully sent to client
notificationsSection.pushRejectedDesc: Send notification when release is rejected
notificationsSection.pushErrorDesc: Send notification when an error occurs while processing

common.regexTooltip1: This field has full regex support (Golang flavour).
common.regexTooltip2: Remember to tick Use Regex if using more than * and ?.

releases.title: Release Names
releases.subtitle: Match only certain release names and/or ignore other release names.
releases.matchWarning: Do you have a good reason to use Match releases instead of one of the other tabs?
releases.exceptWarning: Do you have a good reason to use Except releases instead of one of the other tabs?

groups.title: Groups
groups.subtitle: Match only certain groups and/or ignore other groups.
groups.matchTooltip: Comma separated list of release groups to match.
groups.exceptTooltip: Comma separated list of release groups to ignore (takes priority over Match release groups).

categories.title: Categories
categories.subtitle: Match or exclude categories (if announced)
categories.matchTooltip: Comma separated list of categories to match.
categories.exceptTooltip: Comma separated list of categories to ignore (takes priority over Match categories).

tags.title: Tags
tags.subtitle: Match or exclude tags (if announced)
tags.matchTooltip: Comma separated list of tags to match.
tags.matchLogicTooltip: Logic used to match filter tags.
tags.exceptTooltip: Comma separated list of tags to ignore (takes priority over Match tags).
tags.exceptLogicTooltip: Logic used to match except tags.

uploaders.title: Uploaders
uploaders.subtitle: Match or ignore uploaders (if announced)
uploaders.matchTooltip: Comma separated list of uploaders to match.
uploaders.exceptTooltip: Comma separated list of uploaders to ignore (takes priority over Match uploaders).

origins.title: Origins
origins.subtitle: Match Internals, Scene, P2P, etc. (if announced)

freeleech.title: Freeleech
freeleech.subtitle: Match based off freeleech (if announced)
freeleech.percentTooltip1: Freeleech may be announced as a binary true/false value or as a percentage, depending on the indexer. Use one or the other.
freeleech.percentTooltip2: The Freeleech toggle overrides this field if it is toggled/true.
freeleech.percentTooltip3: Refer to our documentation for more details:
freeleech.toggleDescription: Cannot be used with Freeleech percent. Overrides Freeleech percent if toggled/true.
freeleech.toggleTooltip1: Freeleech may be announced as a binary true/false value or as a percentage, depending on the indexer. Use one or the other.
freeleech.toggleTooltip2: This field overrides Freeleech percent if it is toggled/true.
freeleech.toggleTooltip3: See who uses what in the documentation:

feedSpecific.title: RSS/Torznab/Newznab-specific
feedSpecific.subtitle: These options are only for Feeds such as RSS, Torznab and Newznab
feedSpecific.seedersTooltip: Number of {{type}} as specified by the respective unit. Only for Torznab

rawReleaseTags.title: Raw Release Tags
rawReleaseTags.subtitleSuffix: This is the raw releaseTags string from the announce.
rawReleaseTags.warning: These might not be what you think they are. For very advanced users who know how things are parsed.

moviesTv.title: Movies / Shows
moviesTv.wildcardTooltip: You can use basic filtering like wildcards * or replace single characters with ?
moviesTv.yearsTooltip: This field takes a range of years and/or comma separated single years.

seasonEpisode.title: Seasons, Episodes and Date
seasonEpisode.subtitle: Set season, episode, year, months and day match constraints.
seasonEpisode.seasonsTooltip: See docs for information about how to only grab season packs:
seasonEpisode.episodesTooltip: See docs for information about how to only grab episodes:
seasonEpisode.monthsTooltip: This field takes a range of months and/or comma separated single months.
seasonEpisode.daysTooltip: This field takes a range of days and/or comma separated single days.
seasonEpisode.smartEpisodeDescription: Do not match episodes older than the last one matched.

quality.title: Quality
quality.subtitle: Set resolution, source, codec and related match constraints.
quality.resolutionsTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected resolutions.
quality.sourcesTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected sources.
quality.codecsTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected codecs.
quality.containersTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected containers.
quality.matchHdrTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected HDR designations.
quality.exceptHdrTooltip: Won't match releases which contain any of the selected HDR designations (takes priority over Match HDR).
quality.matchOtherTooltip: Will match releases which contain any of the selected designations.
quality.exceptOtherTooltip: Won't match releases which contain any of the selected Other designations (takes priority over Match Other).

music.wildcardTooltip: You can use basic filtering like wildcards * or replace single characters with ?
music.matchRecordLabelsTooltip: Comma separated list of record labels to match. Only Orpheus and Redacted support this.
music.exceptRecordLabelsTooltip: Comma separated list of record labels to ignore (takes priority over Match record labels). Only Orpheus and Redacted support this.

releaseDetails.title: Release details
releaseDetails.subtitle: Type (Album, Single, EP, etc.) and year of release (if announced)
releaseDetails.musicTypeTooltip: Will only match releases with any of the selected types.
releaseDetails.yearsTooltip: This field takes a range of years and/or comma separated single years.

quality.title: Quality
quality.subtitle: Format, source, log, etc.
quality.formatTooltip: Will only match releases with any of the selected formats. This is overridden by Perfect FLAC.
quality.qualityTooltip: Will only match releases with any of the selected qualities. This is overridden by Perfect FLAC.
quality.mediaTooltip: Will only match releases with any of the selected sources. This is overridden by Perfect FLAC.
quality.logScoreTooltip: Log scores go from 0 to 100. This is overridden by Perfect FLAC.
quality.perfectFlacDescription: Override all options about quality, source, format, and cue/log/log score.
quality.perfectFlacTooltip: Override all options about quality, source, format, and CUE/LOG/LOG score.
quality.perfectFlacHint: This is what you want in 90% of cases (instead of options above).

external.title: External filters
external.subtitle: Run external scripts or webhooks and check status as part of filtering.
external.removeText: Are you sure you want to remove this external filter? This action cannot be undone.
external.sectionSubtitle: Define the type of your filter and its name
external.name: Name
external.typeTooltip: Select the type for this external filter.
external.onErrorTooltip: Select what to do on error for this external filter.

execute.title: Execute
execute.subtitle: Specify the executable, the argument and the expected exit status to run as a pre-filter
execute.pathTooltip: For custom commands you should specify the full path to the binary/program you want to run. And you can include your own static variables:

request.title: Request
request.subtitle: Specify your request destination endpoint, headers and expected return status
request.endpointTooltip: URL or IP to your API. Pass params and set API tokens etc.
request.httpMethodTooltip: Select the HTTP method for this webhook. Defaults to POST

instance.title: Instance
instance.subtitle: Select the specific instance which you want to handle this release filter.

common.numberNoLimit: Takes any number (0 is no limit)
common.skipHashCheckDescription: Add torrent and skip hash check
common.announceSubtitle: Set number of reannounces (if needed), delete after Y announce failures, etc.
common.disableReannounceDescription: Reannounce is enabled by default. Disable if it's not needed
common.reannounceInterval: Reannounce interval. Run every X seconds
common.deleteStalledDescription: Delete stalled torrents after Y attempts

test.text: The test action does nothing except to show if the filter works. Make sure to have your Logs page open while testing.

exec.title: Exec Arguments
exec.subtitle: Specify the executable and its arguments to be executed upon filter match. Use an absolute path.

watchFolder.title: Watch Folder Arguments
watchFolder.subtitle: Point to where autobrr should save the files it fetches. Use an absolute path.

webhook.title: Webhook Arguments
webhook.subtitle: Specify the payload to be sent to the desired endpoint upon filter match.
webhook.endpointTooltip: URL or IP to your API. Pass params and set API tokens etc.

arr.overrideClientName: Override download client name for arr
arr.overrideClientNameTooltip: Override download client name from the one set in Clients. Useful if you have multiple clients inside the arr.
arr.overrideClientId: Override download client id for arr DEPRECATED
arr.overrideClientIdTooltip: Override download client ID from the one set in Clients. Useful if you have multiple clients inside the arr.

qbittorrent.macrosTooltip: The field can use macros to transform/add values from metadata:
qbittorrent.savePathTooltip: Set a custom save path for this action. Automatic Torrent Management will take care of this if using qBittorrent with categories.
qbittorrent.downloadPathTooltip: Set a custom download (incomplete) path for this action. Automatic Torrent Management will take care of this if using qBittorrent with categories.
qbittorrent.ignoreRulesDescription: Choose to ignore rules set in Client Settings.
qbittorrent.priorityTooltip: Torrent Queueing will be enabled for you if it is disabled. Ensure you set your preferred limits for it in your client.
qbittorrent.firstLastPiece: Download first and last pieces first
qbittorrent.firstLastPieceDescription: Add torrent and download first and last pieces first

deluge.label: Label
deluge.labelPlaceholder: eg. label1 (must exist in Deluge to work)
deluge.skipHashCheckTooltip: This will only work on Deluge v2.

rtorrent.label: Label
rtorrent.renameToPath: Do not add torrent name to path

porla.presetTooltip: A case-sensitive preset name as configured in Porla.
